channel.title: CyberWatch Threat Intelligence
channel.description: Aggregated CVEs, advisories, incidents and IOCs

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: python-llvmlite-0.48.0-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-ebbaee3606)
item.description: Automatic update for python-llvmlite-0.48.0-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Fri Jul 3 2026 Benjamin A. Beasley - 0.48.0-1 - Update to 0.48.0 - Closes RHBZ#2453491; fixes RHBZ#2495139; fixes RHBZ#2496401 ```

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: cifs-utils-7.6-2.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-90dbff48ca)
item.description: Automatic update for cifs-utils-7.6-2.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Mon Jul 6 2026 Paulo Alcantara - 7.6-2 - cifs.upcall: fix regression with kerberos mounts - Resolves: rhbz#2497446 - Resolves: rhbz#2496963 - Resolves: rhbz#2489808 - CVE-2026-12505 ```

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: firefox-152.0.4-1.fc43 (FEDORA-2026-410580270b)
item.description: - Updated to latest upstream (152.0.4) ---- Update to latest upstream version

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: upower-1.91.3-1.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-e5305cffc2)
item.description: upower 1.91.3: * Feature: up-device-battery: Prefer "Standard" over "Fast" charging (!316 (merged), #344 (closed)) * Fix: Resolve potential leaks (!327 (merged)) * Fix: Potential out-of-bound access (!328 (merged)) * Fix: Improve access control (!326 (merged)) * Fix: Remove unused codes (!329 (merged)) * Fix: Fix for Asus Battery Charge Threshold Detection (!330 (merged), #347 (closed))

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: openssh-10.2p1-12.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-a80275b42d)
item.description: Improve GSS KEX algorithms documentation ---- - CVE-2026-55653: Fix double free in openssh DH-GEX client path during FIPS known-group validation that leads to client-side denial of service - CVE-2026-55654: Fix heap out-of-bounds read during GSSAPI indicator cleanup due to missing NULL terminator - CVE-2026-55655: Fix MITM of X11 forwarding via abstract UNIX socket pre-binding

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: kind-0.31.0-4.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-c4a4c6a033)
item.description: Automatic update for kind-0.31.0-4.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Tue Jul 7 2026 blinxen - 0.31.0-4 - Rebuild (rhbz#2494326) ```

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: ansible-collection-ansible-posix-2.2.1-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-4a0c61a43f)
item.description: Automatic update for ansible-collection-ansible-posix-2.2.1-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Wed Jul 8 2026 Maxwell G - 2.2.1-1 - Update to 2.2.1. Fixes rhbz#2479556. - Mitigates CVE-2026-11837 (rhbz#2487430, rhbz#2487431, rhbz#2487432) ```

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: python-safetensors-0.8.0-2.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-9db41d0ada)
item.description: Update safetensors to [0.8.0](https://github.com/safetensors/safetensors/releases#release-v0.8.0) and build the Python extension using PyO3 0.29, which fixes RUSTSEC-2026-0176 and RUSTSEC-2026-0177.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: python-uv-build-0.11.28-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-118fdf066a)
item.description: https://github.com/astral-sh/uv/releases/tag/0.11.28

item.title: [HIGH] Fedora: attr-2.6.0-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-c7aafa6056)
item.description: Automatic update for attr-2.6.0-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Thu Jul 9 2026 Lukáš Zaoral - 2.6.0-1 - rebase to the latest upstream release (rhbz#2494157) - CVE-2026-54371 - Symlink Traversal Privilege Escalation via getfattr (rhbz#2494172) - remove a long-overdue workaround for /usr/include/attr/xattr.h ```

item.description: CVE-2026-54520 / GHSA-cm8g-8jfq-887p: ai-agent-automation workflow path traversal advisory landing page

item.description: CVE-2026-53571 `server.fs.deny` bypass on Windows alternate paths PoC.

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-52100
item.description: Public disclosure for CVE-2026-52100 (CSRF) & CVE-2026-52101 (SSRF) in linx-server. MITRE assigned the CVEs; this repo provides a public reference and helps affected users understand the risk.

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2026-40473
item.description: Reproducer for CVE-2026-40473: Apache Camel camel-mina MinaConverter.toObjectInput unsafe deserialization (RCE over TCP/UDP)

item.title: [MEDIUM] VMware: VMSA-2026-0004: VMware Cloud Foundation Operations updates address multiple vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-41722, CVE-2026-41723 and CVE-2026-41724)

item.title: [MEDIUM] Spamhaus DROP: 1.10.16.0/20
item.description: Malicious IP range: 1.10.16.0/20 — SBL256894

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-28979: An out-of-bounds access issue was addressed with improved bounds checking. This ...
item.description: An out-of-bounds access issue was addressed with improved bounds checking. This issue is fixed in Safari 26.5.2, iOS 26.5.2 and iPadOS 26.5.2, macOS Tahoe 26.5.2. Processing maliciously crafted web content may lead to an unexpected process crash.

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-13763: Inconsistent interpretation of HTTP/2 requests in AWS Application Load Balancer ...
item.description:
Inconsistent interpretation of HTTP/2 requests in AWS Application Load Balancer with AWS WAF enabled might allow remote actors to bypass AWS WAF managed rule body inspection via crafted HTTP/2 requests that fragment the request body across frames so that only a partial body is inspected. This issue only impacts HTTP/2 ALB target groups.
To remediate this issue, customers should enable the "Insp

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-13762: Inconsistent interpretation of HTTP/2 requests in Amazon CloudFront with AWS WAF...
item.description:
Inconsistent interpretation of HTTP/2 requests in Amazon CloudFront with AWS WAF enabled might allow remote actors to bypass AWS WAF managed rule body inspection via crafted HTTP/2 requests that fragment the request body across frames so that only a partial body is inspected.
This issue was remediated server-side. No customer action is required.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-13593: CSS::Minifier::XS versions before 0.14 for Perl have a memory leak when the enti...
item.description:
CSS::Minifier::XS versions before 0.14 for Perl have a memory leak when the entire document is minified away.
The minify function has a memory leak when processing a document containing only characters to be removed, such as comments and whitespace.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-13008: Rejected reason: ** REJECT ** DO NOT USE THIS CANDIDATE NUMBER. ConsultIDs: CVE-...
item.description: Rejected reason: ** REJECT ** DO NOT USE THIS CANDIDATE NUMBER. ConsultIDs: CVE-2026-57700. Reason: This candidate is a reservation duplicate of CVE-2026-57700. Notes: All CVE users should reference CVE-2026-57700 instead of this candidate. All references and descriptions in this candidate have been removed to prevent accidental usage.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-58000: luci-proto-openvpn through 0.11.1, fixed in commit e4ff45e, contains a command i...
item.description: luci-proto-openvpn through 0.11.1, fixed in commit e4ff45e, contains a command injection vulnerability in the generateKey ubus method where the cl_meta parameter is interpolated into a shell command without proper escaping or quoting. An authenticated LuCI user with OpenVPN protocol configuration access can inject arbitrary shell metacharacters into cl_meta to execute commands as root via the pope

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-57999: luci-app-tailscale-community contains a command injection vulnerability in the t...
item.description: luci-app-tailscale-community contains a command injection vulnerability in the tailscale.do_login RPC method that allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands as root. The vulnerability exists because user-controlled loginserver and loginserver_authkey parameters are improperly quoted within a double-quoted shell command, allowing shell substitutions like $() to be evaluated by the out

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-53428: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value vulnerability in leandrocp mdex allo...
item.description:
Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value vulnerability in leandrocp mdex allows an unauthenticated attacker to cause a denial of service through unbounded memory allocation.
comrak_nif::lumis_adapter::LumisAdapter::parse_highlight_lines in native/comrak_nif/src/lumis_adapter.rs eagerly expands a user-controlled inclusive line range from a fenced code block's highlight_lines decorator into a Ve

item.title: [HIGH] CVE-2026-53427: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripti...
item.description:
Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in leandrocp MDEx allows stored or reflected cross-site scripting via attacker-controlled Markdown.
When syntax highlighting and full info-string forwarding (render: [full_info_string: true]) are enabled, the Lumis adapter copies the value of a code fence's highlight_lines_class info-string attribut

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-13757: A flaw was found in p11-kit. The RPC message attribute parsing functions p11_rpc...
item.description: A flaw was found in p11-kit. The RPC message attribute parsing functions p11_rpc_message_get_attribute() and p11_rpc_message_get_attribute_array_value() form a mutually-recursive call chain with no recursion depth limit when processing nested CKA_WRAP_TEMPLATE, CKA_UNWRAP_TEMPLATE, and CKA_DERIVE_TEMPLATE attributes. An unauthenticated attacker with local access to the p11-kit RPC Unix domain sock

item.title: [MEDIUM] Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description: Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API. "Operators rely on automated scraping tooling with custom or legitimate-sounding user agents, leveraging GitHub 'ghost' accounts that are often years old, or compromised OAuth tokens and personal

item.title: [HIGH] New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as commands the operator can choose from. Each is a different way to break a machine: wipe the whole disk, overwrite the Windows drive, or run fake "ransomware" that scrambles files with a key it never saves

item.title: [MEDIUM] Winning 54% of the time
item.description: With Wimbledon's help, Hazel argues against the popular myth that "Attackers only need to be right once, but defenders need to be right 100% of the time."

item.title: [MEDIUM] New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
item.description: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. [...]

item.title: [MEDIUM] Microsoft expects more Windows security updates from AI-discovered flaws
item.description: Microsoft says Windows users should expect to see an increase in security updates as the company increasingly relies on artificial intelligence to discover vulnerabilities in its codebase. [...]

item.title: [LOW] npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description: GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA). The Microsoft-owned subsidiary noted that the following npm install behaviors that used to run automatically before have been made opt-in - allowScripts defaults to off, meaning

item.title: [LOW] ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
item.description: Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch it. This week is full of that kind of damage. Not loud. Not clever. Just small gaps doing big jobs. The worst part is how normal it all looks until the bill arrives. The full ThreatsDay list is below. Global

item.title: [MEDIUM] QIZ Security Raises $17 Million for Cryptographic Governance Platform
item.description: The Israeli company has developed a cryptographic posture and post-quantum cryptography management platform. The post QIZ Security Raises $17 Million for Cryptographic Governance Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [MEDIUM] New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 focuses on stealing Microsoft 365 accounts by combining adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code methods with AI-assisted lure generation. [...]

item.title: [MEDIUM] As Global Conflicts Go Digital, Businesses Need Wartime Gameplans
item.description: The fate of a Ukrainian tax software company shows how modern cyberwarfare can claim casualties far beyond the battlefield, and how businesses across the ocean still need to protect themselves.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Suspected Russian Threat Actor Impersonates Legitimate Crypto Wallets to Deploy Remote Utilities
item.description: submitted by   /u/CyberMasterV [link]   [comments]
